title: GameStop Fiscal Year 2024 Results Summary
text: GameStop reported mixed results for Q4 2024 (ended February 1, 2025). Net sales declined to $1.283 billion from $1.794 billion year-over-year, representing a 28% decrease. However, the company achieved net income of $131.3 million compared to $63.1 million in the prior year quarter. Operating efficiency improved, with SG&A expenses at $282.5 million for the fourth quarter, compared to $359.2 million in the prior year's fourth quarter. Adjusted EBITDA reached $96.5 million versus $88.0 million previously. For fiscal 2024, net sales totaled $3.823 billion, down significantly from $5.273 billion in fiscal 2023. The company reported full-year net income of $131.3 million compared to $6.7 million the prior year—a substantial improvement despite lower revenues. Notably, adjusted EBITDA declined to $36.1 million from $64.7 million year-over-year. GameStop substantially strengthened its financial position, ending the year with cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities of $4.775 billion at the close of the quarter. This dramatic increase was driven by at-the-market share offerings generating $3.454 billion in proceeds. The company completed the divestiture of Italian operations and wound down German store operations during the period.
